Visit WWW.IOWATAXANDTAGS.ORG to renew your registration online and
avoid the wait! It's easy, convenient, and secure. Renewals completed online
are mailed out the next business day. Public Service Hours: 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM.
Open 9:00 AM - 4:30 PM on the third Thursday of every month.
Vehicle title transfers are by appointment only. Appointments for any service may
be scheduled at https://www.polkcountylowa.gov/treasurer/appointments/.
Renew online with an electronic check,
credit card or debit card.
www.lowaTaxAndTags.org

PENALTY AND ENFORCEMENT DATE WILL BEGIN: May 1, 2026
Penalty and Enforcement: Vehicles may be renewed one month prior to your registration renewal month, your renewal
month, or the month following without penalty. Penalty for late payment is assessed the first day of the second month following
your registration month. If the last day to renew your vehicle(s) falls on a Saturday, Sunday or Holiday, you have until the next
business day to renew without late penalty. Listed above and below is your "Penalty and Enforcement Date".
A Service Charge will be assessed for any returned check.
The 'tax due' includes registration taxes, special plate fees, and postage & handling of $1.00 for each validation sticker
issued or $5.00 for each set of plates. A "Y" in the PLATES column indicates a new plate will be issued.
PLEASE CHECK YOUR DRIVER'S LICENSE FOR EXPIRATION

ai_result.summary: This document is a vehicle registration renewal notice issued by the Polk County Treasurer to Joel Patrick Kruse. It details the renewal for two vehicles: a 2014 Honda and a 2014 Subaru. The total amount due for the renewal is $92.00, which will register the vehicles through March 2027. The penalty and enforcement date for late payment is May 1, 2026. The notice provides instructions for online payment using a specific PIN and includes a detachable payment stub.
ai_result.reasoning: The document is categorized as a vehicle registration renewal based on its explicit title and content. It includes all standard elements of such a notice: recipient information, vehicle details (make, year, VIN, plate number), tax amounts, a total due, a penalty date, and instructions for payment to a government treasury office.
